title: Stegnogramma asplenioides
taxonomy: Stegnogramma asplenioides is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns. It belongs to the order Polypodiales, which consists of various types of ferns. At the family level, Thelypteridaceae is part of the larger group of plants known as Pteridophyta, or vascular cryptogams. The genus Stegnogramma is further classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Stegnogramma asplenioides, also known as the ladder fern, is a type of flowering plant in the family Thelypteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties.
